column1: # LoM Thai Text — PoC probe set (Solution 2)
# format: key<TAB>value   ·   UTF-8, no BOM, LF   ·   '#' = comment
# escapes in the value: \n \t \\
# Every line here is a *key*, not a Chinese string. Nothing in this file can be
# expressed by the XUnity text-keyed dictionary.
# v2 — the "[S2]" markers are GONE. They were the bug: a title button is sized for
# two Chinese glyphs, so "[S2] " filled line 1 and the Thai wrapped to line 2 and got
# clipped. Values here are deliberately short.
# --- A. title screen. Three short labels + ONE deliberately long one as a control.
#     If the three short ones read Thai and 'Setting' shows a clipped/shrunken mess,
#     the box-size diagnosis is confirmed and the fix is UI sizing, not fonts.
System/Start/StartGame
System/Start/Library
System/Start/Exit
System/Start/Setting
column2: ตั้งค่าระบบทั้งหมดของเกม

column1: # --- B. .NET format template. Game does string.Format(this, year, month, stage).
#     "第{0}年{1}{2}" — infinite variants, so the dictionary could never match it.
#     Already confirmed working on the Load Game panel.
System/GameTimeText
# --- C. single-character values — the class we had to COMMENT OUT in v1.2 because
#     唐=ถัง partial-substituted into 三师兄. Here there is no text matching at all.
#     Vertical two-character buttons on the 养成 screen.
System/Title/Training_01
System/Title/Training_02
System/Title/Forge_01
System/Title/Forge_02
# --- D. collision divergence. Both keys hold the SAME Chinese text 事件, so the
#     dictionary is forced to give them one identical Thai rendering forever.
#     Here they diverge. Kept short so the box cannot clip them.
Middle/Title/Event
System/StoryEvent
